success_surface: Successfully applied lookup formulas to a cell range using INDEX/MATCH with proper reference handling and saved to new output file
effective_behaviors: Selected INDEX/MATCH formula pattern for complex conditional lookups instead of nested IF statements
Programmatically iterated through target cell range to apply consistent formula logic across multiple rows
Used absolute references for lookup table ranges while maintaining relative references for dynamic lookup keys
Applied openpyxl library for direct formula injection into cells without evaluation
Preserved file integrity by writing output to separate file path rather than modifying source in-place
evidence: Chose '=INDEX($B$1:$B$4,MATCH($A'+str(i)+',$A$1:$A$4,0))' over nested IF approach for cleaner maintainability
Looped through range(14, 18) to dynamically build cell addresses and formulas for B14:B17
Dollar signs properly anchored lookup ranges ($B$1:$B$4, $A$1:$A$4) while A column references remained flexible ($A14)
Used openpyxl load_workbook and ws[cell].value = formula to write unevaluated formulas
Saved to '/mnt/data/outputs/.../output.xlsx' rather than overwriting original init.xlsx file

memory_items.title: Lookup Formula Preference
memory_items.lesson: For multi-row lookups with mapping tables, INDEX/MATCH provides more maintainable code than nested IF statements
memory_items.generalization: When implementing conditional value mappings across cell ranges, prefer INDEX/MATCH or VLOOKUP patterns over deeply nested logical functions

memory_items.title: Range Iteration Pattern
memory_items.lesson: Programmatic loops eliminate repetitive code when applying formulas to contiguous cell ranges
memory_items.generalization: Use iterative approaches to apply consistent formula logic across multiple adjacent cells rather than coding each cell individually

memory_items.title: Absolute Reference Anchoring
memory_items.lesson: Lock lookup table references with absolute notation while keeping lookup key references flexible
memory_items.generalization: When writing formulas to a range, anchor static table/range references with absolute notation while preserving relative positioning for dynamic elements

memory_items.title: Library Selection for Formulas
memory_items.lesson: openpyxl directly supports formula string injection without automatic evaluation
memory_items.generalization: Select openpyxl when the task requires inserting spreadsheet formulas that must remain unevaluated as cell expressions

memory_items.title: File Integrity Preservation
memory_items.lesson: Original workbooks should never be modified in-place; output to a new file
memory_items.generalization: Always preserve source file integrity by writing results to a separate output file unless explicit overwrite permission is given

edits.content:
## 6. Trace-Derived Lessons
- **Lookup Formula Selection**: For conditional value mappings across multiple rows, INDEX/MATCH patterns provide more maintainable solutions than nested IF statements.
- **Range Iteration Strategy**: Use programmatic loops to apply consistent formula logic across contiguous cell ranges instead of addressing each cell individually.
- **Reference Anchoring Rules**: When copying formulas across a range, anchor static lookup table references with absolute notation ($A$1:$A$4) while keeping lookup key references flexibly positioned ($A14).
- **Formula Library Guidance**: Prefer openpyxl when tasks require inserting spreadsheet formulas as unevaluated strings rather than computed values.
- **Source File Protection**: Always write manipulated outputs to a new file path; never modify the original workbook in-place.
